what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600x900 HTML composition titled "Gen3 — the banded stack (v2)" with a seasonal ledger theme, a horizontal metrics band, three seasonal plates, inset numeric callouts, a generated detail strip, and runtime-injected icons.
observations: Uses Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts plus shared CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css.

Builds a centered fixed-size artboard with radial background color, faint blueprint grid corners, a title block, a goldwash band, three cards, a lower detail strip, a circular V mark, and footer wash.
The top band shows six metrics: 9 seams opened, 1:1 drawn to size, 365 days in order, 3 hands at the bench, 12h between the tides, and 1,280° at the peak.
The three plates are Spring · the Cut, Summer · the Drawing, and Winter · the Keeping, each with an icon placeholder, inset measurement, and three short action fragments.
JavaScript depends on a global DNA.org runtime to render scale, compass, and ledger icons and a detail strip containing months and ledger phrases.
A comment names the inset as an exercise in recursion with a provision for nesting_depth.
standouts: Seasonal structure is expressed as three large plates under a shared metrics band.
Ledger and craft language are carried through titles, measurements, fragments, and detail strip text.
The file combines static layout CSS with small runtime organism calls for icons and detail strip generation.
conventions: Uses inline page-local CSS and JavaScript inside a standalone HTML file.
Relies heavily on shared design tokens for colors, spacing, radius, and type sizes.
Uses BEM-like short class names rather than semantic section elements.
Keeps the composition fixed to a 1600x900 viewport instead of responsive sizing.
Uses id-based DOM injection for decorative organisms.
concerns: The fixed 1600x900 artboard may not adapt to smaller or larger viewports.
The script assumes DNA.org exists and does not guard against missing runtime dependencies.
The visual meaning of several numeric metrics is not explained inside the file.
Inline CSS and JavaScript make reuse or testing of individual parts less direct.
notes: This file is a self-contained visual ledger composition for a seasonal banded stack, using shared tokens and runtime-generated ornamental details.
questions: Is the 1600x900 canvas intended as the only target size?
What does the circular "V" mark denote?
Are the six top-band metrics literal data, symbolic labels, or sample content?
What is the intended meaning of nesting_depth mentioned in the inset comment?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and content align: the file presents a banded stack composition organized around seasonal movements and ledger-like measurements.
intention_signals: It appears intended to translate a year of craft or production into a structured visual ledger, with seasonal plates nested beneath a shared band of measurements.
novelty: The composition treats metrics, seasonal cards, inset callouts, and month-strip detail as one layered ledger-like visual system.
contribution: Contributes a fixed artboard pattern for presenting seasonal process data as a banded ledger: shared metric strip, three stage plates, inset numeric summaries, small generated icons, and a generated detail strip.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML/CSS/JS artboard rendering a Gen3 seasonal banded stack with a metrics band, three process plates, inset ledger numbers, runtime icons, and a detail strip.
intention_for_embedding: Designed to express seasonal craft movements and ledger keeping as a structured visual substrate with nested measures and shared design-token styling.
